source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Cloud Storage Buckets Have Uniform Bucket- Level Access Enabled

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud Storage Buckets Have Uniform Bucket- Level Access Enabled
input_row.description: It is recommended that uniform bucket-level access is enabled on Cloud Storage buckets.
input_row.rationale: It is recommended to use uniform bucket-level access to unify and simplify how you grant access to your Cloud Storage resources. Cloud Storage offers two systems for granting users permission to access your buckets and objects: Cloud Identity and Access Management (Cloud IAM) and Access Control Lists (ACLs). These systems act in parallel - in order for a user to access a Cloud Storage resource, only one of the systems needs to grant the user permission. Cloud IAM is used throughout Google Cloud and allows you to grant a variety of permissions at the bucket and project levels. ACLs are used only by Cloud Storage and have limited permission options, but they allow you to grant permissions on a per-object basis. In order to support a uniform permissioning system, Cloud Storage has uniform bucket- level access. Using this feature disables ACLs for all Cloud Storage resources: access to Cloud Storage resources then is granted exclusively through Cloud IAM. Enabling uniform bucket-level access guarantees that if a Storage bucket is not publicly accessible, no object in the bucket is publicly accessible either. Impact: If you enable uniform bucket-level access, you revoke access from users who gain their access solely through object ACLs. Certain Google Cloud services, such as Stackdriver, Cloud Audit Logs, and Datastore, cannot export to Cloud Storage buckets that have uniform bucket-level access enabled.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Open the Cloud Storage browser in the Google Cloud Console by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser 2. For each bucket, make sure that Access control column has the value Uniform. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all buckets in a project gsutil ls 2. For each bucket, verify that uniform bucket-level access is enabled. gsutil uniformbucketlevelaccess get gs://BUCKET_NAME/ If uniform bucket-level access is enabled, the response looks like: Uniform bucket-level access setting for gs://BUCKET_NAME/: Enabled: True LockedTime: LOCK_DATE
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Open the Cloud Storage browser in the Google Cloud Console by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser 2. In the list of buckets, click on the name of the desired bucket. 3. Select the Permissions tab near the top of the page. 4. In the text box that starts with This bucket uses fine-grained access control..., click Edit. 5. In the pop-up menu that appears, select Uniform. 6. Click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Use the on option in a uniformbucketlevelaccess set command: gsutil uniformbucketlevelaccess set on gs://BUCKET_NAME/ Prevention You can set up an Organization Policy to enforce that any new bucket has uniform bucket level access enabled. Learn more at: https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/setting-org-policies#uniform-bucket Default Value: By default, Cloud Storage buckets do not have uniform bucket-level access enabled. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/uniform-bucket-level-access 2. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/using-uniform-bucket-level-access 3. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/setting-org-policies#uniform-bucket Additional Information: Uniform bucket-level access can no longer be disabled if it has been active on a bucket for 90 consecutive days.
input_row.impact: If you enable uniform bucket-level access, you revoke access from users who gain their access solely through object ACLs. Certain Google Cloud services, such as Stackdriver, Cloud Audit Logs, and Datastore, cannot export to Cloud Storage buckets that have uniform bucket-level access enabled.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/uniform-bucket-level-access 2. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/using-uniform-bucket-level-access 3. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/setting-org-policies#uniform-bucket Additional Information: Uniform bucket-level access can no longer be disabled if it has been active on a bucket for 90 consecutive days.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control regarding ensuring that Cloud Storage buckets have uniform bucket-level access enabled can be fully verified through GCP APIs, Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), and GCP CLI commands. This makes it an ideal candidate for automation because:

✓ GCP APIs can query this property: The Google Cloud SDK and GCP CLI provide direct commands to check the uniform bucket-level access setting on Cloud Storage buckets.

✓ Standard GCP service property: This control deals with access control configurations, which is a standard and API-exposed feature of Cloud Storage.

✓ Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) methods exist: The audit field explicitly mentions the use of the `gsutil` command, which can check the uniform bucket-level access setting for each bucket.

✓ GCP CLI commands available: The gsutil CLI includes commands like `gsutil uniformbucketlevelaccess get gs://BUCKET_NAME/` to retrieve the current setting.

✓ The audit mentions Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands: The audit procedure provides clear instructions using the CLI, ensuring it's supported by the SDK.

Given all the above points, this control is best approached with automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_storage_bucket_uniform_access_level_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP CLI Command:** Use the `gsutil uniformbucketlevelaccess get gs://BUCKET_NAME/` command for each Cloud Storage bucket to verify the uniform bucket-level access setting.
- **API Call:** Consider using the `storage.buckets.getIamPolicy` method from the Storage JSON API to confirm bucket-level IAM policy settings if you extend beyond gsutil.
- **Configuration Property:** Check the "Uniform bucket-level access" setting.
- **Expected Values:** Valid response indicating that "Enabled: True" is present in the uniform bucket-level access setting.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** The response for each bucket confirms "Enabled: True" for uniform bucket-level access.
  - **Fail:** Any bucket returns "Enabled: False" or the response does not include the relevant settings.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** All Cloud Storage buckets within the project need to be audited.
- **Process:** Enumerate all buckets for a given project, check each bucket's uniform access control configuration, and log buckets that do not meet the compliance criteria.

This automated approach efficiently ensures that all buckets adhere to the recommended security setting by using readily available tools and APIs.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. Uniform bucket-level access is a well-defined, API-exposed property on GCS buckets. There is no need for manual review unless there are exceptional circumstances (e.g., API access issues).
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Coverage:**  
  The control can be fully checked via:
    - **gsutil**: `gsutil uniformbucketlevelaccess get gs://BUCKET_NAME/`
    - **gcloud**: `gcloud storage buckets describe gs://BUCKET_NAME --format="value(uniformBucketLevelAccess.enabled)"`
    - **REST API**: `GET https://storage.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b/BUCKET_NAME?fields=iamConfiguration`
  - **CloudFormation** is AWS-specific and not applicable.  
  - **GCP Config** (now called Config Controller) does not natively monitor this property, but can be extended with custom policies.
- **Conclusion:**  
  The control is fully automatable using standard GCP tooling.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:**
    - **gsutil** command is correct and supported.
    - **gcloud** command is not mentioned but should be, as it is more consistent with GCP's preferred CLI.
    - **API Call:** The mention of `storage.buckets.getIamPolicy` is **incorrect** for this property. The correct API is `storage.buckets.get` with the `iamConfiguration` field. `getIamPolicy` only returns IAM bindings, not the uniform bucket-level access setting.
    - **Configuration Property:** Correctly identifies "Uniform bucket-level access".
    - **Pass/Fail Criteria:** Correct.
    - **Resource Enumeration:** Correct.
    - **Program Name:** Follows convention but could be more specific (see below).
- **Missing/Incorrect Details:**
    - Should mention the `gcloud` command for completeness.
    - Should clarify that `getIamPolicy` is not the correct API for this check.
    - Could include REST API example for completeness.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **API Method Correction:**  
  Replace `storage.buckets.getIamPolicy` with `storage.buckets.get` (with `iamConfiguration` field).
- **Add gcloud Command:**  
  Include:  
  ```
  gcloud storage buckets describe gs://BUCKET_NAME --format="value(uniformBucketLevelAccess.enabled)"
  ```
- **Program Name:**  
  Current: `gcp_storage_bucket_uniform_access_level_enforced`  
  Suggested: `gcp_storage_bucket_uniform_bucket_level_access_enabled`  
  (More descriptive and matches the GCP property name.)
- **Additional Considerations:**
    - For large orgs, recommend using [Cloud Asset Inventory](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/overview) to enumerate all buckets across projects.
    - Note that the property is now called "Uniform bucket-level access" (legacy term: "Uniform access control").
    - Consider logging buckets where the property is unset (default is disabled).
    - Mention that automation should handle pagination if there are many buckets.
- **Alternative API:**  
  Use `GET https://storage.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b/BUCKET_NAME?fields=iamConfiguration` to directly check the property.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is fundamentally sound, with minor corrections needed for API method and completeness.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Modify** the automation details to:
    - Replace `storage.buckets.getIamPolicy` with `storage.buckets.get` (with `iamConfiguration`).
    - Add the `gcloud` command as a supported method.
    - Update the program name for clarity.
    - Optionally, mention Cloud Asset Inventory for large-scale enumeration.

---

## **Summary Table**

| Section              | Assessment                                                                                   |
|----------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation  | AGREE                                                                                        |
| Technical Accuracy   | 8/10 (API method correction, add gcloud command)                                             |
| Improvements         | Correct API method, add gcloud, clarify program name, suggest Cloud Asset Inventory          |
| Confidence           | HIGH                                                                                         |
| Final Recommendation | MODIFY (as above)                                                                            |

---

**References:**
- [GCP Storage Buckets: Uniform bucket-level access](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/uniform-bucket-level-access)
- [gcloud storage buckets describe](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/storage/buckets/describe)
- [Storage Buckets API: GET](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/get)

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identified that the uniform bucket-level access setting for Cloud Storage buckets is fully exposed via GCP APIs and can be programmatically checked using both `gsutil` and `gcloud` commands, as well as the REST API. The property is standard, well-documented, and does not require any manual inspection or console-only actions. This control is a textbook case for automation.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_storage_bucket_uniform_bucket_level_access_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Cloud Storage Buckets
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - Google Cloud SDK (gcloud):  
    `gcloud storage buckets describe BUCKET_NAME --format="value(uniformBucketLevelAccess.enabled)"`
  - gsutil:  
    `gsutil uniformbucketlevelaccess get gs://BUCKET_NAME`
  - REST API:  
    `GET https://storage.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b/BUCKET_NAME?fields=iamConfiguration`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - `uniformBucketLevelAccess.enabled` (gcloud/REST API)
  - Output from `gsutil uniformbucketlevelaccess get`
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - The property `uniformBucketLevelAccess.enabled` is `True` (or output states "enabled: True" or equivalent).
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - The property `uniformBucketLevelAccess.enabled` is `False` (or output states "enabled: False" or equivalent).

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the two AI opinions. The second opinion provided a more technically precise API reference (noting the correct REST field and clarifying that `getIamPolicy` is not relevant), which is reflected in the final automation details.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
